locations_inspected.name: Hill Country Hospitality Partners, LLC
locations_inspected.construction: TPO Roof; Gable Roof Geometry; Roof Deck Attachment B - Nails (6" o.c.)
findings: Roof type: TPO; Roof age: 16 years; Estimated remaining useful life: 0 years per Roofing section
Roof Condition Rating section indicates Overall Roof Condition: Excellent with estimated remaining useful life of 7 years (contradicts Roofing section)
Photographic documentation: IMG-4929 (rear of building), IMG-8104 (front/street view), IMG-8696 (storage area/housekeeping), IMG-3385 (roof aerial/ladder view)
Report approved by D. Moore on 2026-06-24
deficiencies: Multiple areas of ponding water observed on flat roof sections [Code violation]
Visible roof deck sagging and water damage indicating potential structural compromise
Electrical panel clearance obstructed by stored material; minimum 36-inch clearance not maintained per NEC Article 110.26
Hood suppression system in kitchen area has expired; annual inspection overdue per NFPA 96 Section 12.2
Exterior lighting inadequate in parking area and loading areas; below minimum 1 foot-candle at ground level
HVAC rooftop units show signs of deferred maintenance; no preventive maintenance program in place
Hot work permit program not formally documented per NFPA 51B

recommendations.description: Visible roof deck sagging and water damage indicate potential structural failure; engage structural engineer for immediate assessment and restrict access to affected areas
Electrical panel clearance obstructed by stored material; maintain minimum 36-inch clearance in front of all electrical panels per NEC Article 110.26
Hood suppression system in kitchen area has expired; schedule annual inspection of kitchen hood suppression system per NFPA 96 Section 12.2
Exterior lighting inadequate in parking area and loading areas; add additional lighting to maintain minimum 1 foot-candle at ground level in all exterior areas
HVAC rooftop units show signs of deferred maintenance; establish quarterly preventive maintenance program for all mechanical equipment
Hot work permit program not formally documented; implement written hot work permit program per NFPA 51B and train all affected personnel

overall_assessment.summary: Roof Condition Certification Report for a 16-year-old TPO roof system at a hotel property (SIC 7011). The Roofing section rates the roof condition as Severe with 0 years of estimated remaining useful life, while the Roof Condition Rating section contradictorily states Excellent condition with 7 years remaining. Notable deficiencies include ponding water on flat roof sections, poor decking, parapet wall cracking, and a critical finding of roof deck sagging with water damage requiring immediate structural assessment. Additional non-roof deficiencies identified include electrical panel obstruction, expired kitchen hood suppression system, inadequate exterior lighting, deferred HVAC maintenance, and an undocumented hot work permit program. The property carries an ISO PPC 5 risk grade. Report was approved by D. Moore on June 24, 2026.
